cases.title: Dot-completion on table name - unqualified columns in schema order

expected.comment: Dot-completion returns unqualified column names in schema order.

cases.title: Dot-completion on alias - unqualified columns in schema order
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM users u WHERE u.|

expected.comment: Dot-completion on alias 'u' returns unqualified columns.

cases.title: Dot-completion with prefix - filtered unqualified columns

expected.comment: Dot-completion with prefix 'c' filters to created_at.

cases.title: Dot-completion - no functions, no keywords, no CURRENT_TABLE logic

expected.comment: Dot-completion ignores CURRENT_TABLE. Only orders columns.

cases.title: Dot-completion on CTE alias
cases.sql: WITH au AS (SELECT id, name FROM users) SELECT au.|

expected.comment: Dot-completion on CTE alias returns CTE columns.

cases.title: Dot-completion on derived table alias
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM (SELECT id, total FROM orders) AS o WHERE o.|

expected.comment: Dot-completion on derived table alias returns derived columns.

cases.title: KILLER: Dot-completion in WHERE on alias - zero functions contamination
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM users u WHERE u.|

expected.comment: KILLER: Dot-completion returns ONLY unqualified columns in schema order. NO functions, NO keywords, NO CURRENT_TABLE logic.

cases.title: KILLER: Dot-completion with prefix filter - startswith post-dot

expected.comment: KILLER: Dot-completion with prefix 'i' filters to 'id' only. Unqualified.

cases.title: KILLER: Dot-completion in SELECT - no functions even if context normally allows

expected.comment: KILLER: Even in SELECT_LIST context, dot-completion returns ONLY columns. NO functions like UPPER, UUID, etc.
